description: generate paired synthetic CT volumes + masks via NV-Generate-CTMR rflow-ct inference (GPU)
rules: thin wrapper: inference/sampling/decoding are delegated to upstream scripts.inference — do not reimplement
preflight runs on every invocation (config bounds, anatomy + body_region validation, FOV minimums, dataset + CUDA presence, VRAM/wall-time estimate)
outputs (image/label NIfTI pairs + result.json) are written under record_store; the upstream clone is never mutated (configs are staged under the output dir)
NV_GENERATE_ROOT must point at a cloned NV-Generate-CTMR repo with downloaded rflow-ct weights and mask-candidate datasets
CONFIG_INFER override keys are restricted (num_output_samples, body_region, anatomy_list, controllable_anatomy_size, output_size, spacing, ...); pass 'default' for the upstream config verbatim
usage: Set CONFIG_INFER (override JSON path or 'default') and NV_GENERATE_ROOT. Output: result.json (schema-validated) plus sample_*_image.nii.gz / sample_*_label.nii.gz pairs under record_store.
limitation: Thin wrapper — do not modify code under NV_GENERATE_ROOT. rflow-ct requires CUDA and ~16 GB VRAM minimum for the default 256^3 output_size; larger output_size needs an A100/H100; there is no CPU fallback. Output volumes are synthetic and not safe as training data for production medtech models without independent quality review. Not for clinical deployment, interpretation, autonomous diagnosis, or regulatory submission.
